According to the‚Äã author, ________‚Äã is(are) making a reasoned analysis of an‚Äã opportunity, envisioning potential‚Äã solutions, evaluating those‚Äã possibilities, and developing the most promising ones.

production

Project management has four phases. Select the list below that contains the correct four phases.
Question content area bottom
Part 1
A.
‚ÄãStarting, discovering,‚Äã doing, and finalizing
B.

One of the following is‚Äã data, the rest are information. Which is‚Äã data?
Question content area bottom
Part 1
A.
Yesterday John ran 4 miles.
B.
For every 100 runners who average 5 miles per‚Äã day, 39 will suffer a stress injury that keeps them from running for at least 8 weeks.
C.
327 of 536 runners finished the marathon last weekend and they averaged 4 hours and 29 minutes to completion.
D.
Last‚Äã year, John ran an average of 
‚ÄÉ‚ÄÉmiles per day.
E.
More mothers buy Salty Shell Peanut Butter.
A. Yesterday John ran 4 miles.

Information exists under which of the following‚Äã conditions?
Question content area bottom
Part 1
A.
Information exists if it is data presented in a meaningful context.
B.
It is information as long as it is derived from data.
C.
Information exists if it is data processed by‚Äã summing, ordering,‚Äã averaging, grouping,‚Äã comparing, or other similar operations.
D.
Information exists only in the mind of a human observer.
E.
Information exists if it is a difference that makes a difference.
A. Information exists if it is data presented in a meaningful context.

Which of the following is NOT a factor in determining if teamwork is perceived as meaningful and‚Äã satisfying?
Question content area bottom
Part 1
A.
If one person is given all the important tasks
B.
If the task they are working on is perceived by the team as important
C.
If an‚Äã individual's work is perceived as important to the team
D.
If each person feels they are part of the group
E.
If the person doing that work is given credit for it
A. If one person is given all the important tasks

One could make the statement that information systems‚Äã (IS) are much more than information technology‚Äã (IT). Why?
Question content area bottom
Part 1
A.
IT is just hardware and software.
B.
IS includes‚Äã factors, components, and processes you‚Äã can't buy over the counter.
C.
IT‚Äã doesn't include software.
D.
IS‚Äã doesn't incorporate technology. IS and IT are unrelated.
E.
People use‚Äã information, they‚Äã don't truly use technology.
B. IS includes‚Äã factors, components, and processes you‚Äã can't buy over the counter.

What are the components of an information system‚Äã (in their correct‚Äã order)?
Question content area bottom
Part 1
A.
‚ÄãHardware, software,‚Äã data, procedures, and users
B.
‚ÄãHardware, software,‚Äã data, procedures, and people
C.
‚ÄãHardware, software,‚Äã data, processes, and people
D.
‚ÄãHardware, software,‚Äã data, and people
E.
‚ÄãHardware, software,‚Äã data, processes, and users
A. Hardware, software, data, procedures, and users

Which of the following is a characteristic of quality‚Äã data?
Question content area bottom
Part 1
A.
Quality data is always expensive.
B.
Quality data is often unavailable when you need it.
C.
It is just barely sufficient to satisfy the need for it.
D.
Quality data is easy to acquire.
E.
Quality data is relatable to any number of‚Äã problems/opportunities.
E. Quality data is relatable to any number of problems/opportunities.

Systems thinking is an important nonroutine skill that will help you achieve job security in the future. Systems thinking is the ability to‚Äã ________.
Question content area bottom
Part 1
A.
model a system
B.
observe the environment as a complex mix of interrelated systems that no one person can completely understand
C.
model the components of the system to ensure others can understand and replicate them
D.
model the components of the system to connect the inputs and outputs among those components into a sensible whole that reflects the structure and dynamics of the phenomenon observed
E.
model the components of the system to connect the inputs and outputs among those components into a reasonably understandable whole
D. model the components of the system to connect the inputs and outputs among those components into a sensible whole that reflects the structure and dynamics of the phenomenon observed

In‚Äã collaboration, work‚Äã (i.e., a document‚Äã draft) proceeds in a series of steps in which one person produces‚Äã something, others criticize‚Äã it, and then a revised version is produced. These steps are known as‚Äã ________.
Question content area bottom
Part 1
A.
redundancies
B.
strategies
C.
alternatives

iterations
D. iterations

n‚Äã 1972, Gordon Bell formulated‚Äã Bell's Law, which states‚Äã ________.
Question content area bottom
Part 1

a new computer class forms roughly each decade establishing a new social hierarchy
B.
a new computer class forms roughly each decade establishing a new industry
C.
a new computer course forms roughly each decade establishing new programs
D.
a new computer class forms roughly each decade
E.
a new computer industry forms roughly each decade establishing a new class
E. a new computer industry forms roughly each decade establishing a new class

‚ÄãMetcalfe's Law states that the value of a network is‚Äã ________.
Question content area bottom
Part 1
A.
equal to the number of nodes X the square root of network traffic
B.
equal to F‚Äã * N‚Äã * C where F‚Äã = use‚Äã frequency, N‚Äã = number of‚Äã users, and C‚Äã = the common purpose of the network members
C.
incalculable
D.
a function of how much ecommerce is generated through it
E.
equal to the square of the number of people connected to it
E. equal to the square of the number of people connected to it
